title: ice40_wrapcarry does not appear to respect (* keep *) attribute on SB_CARRY cells
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

I am attempting to do experiments with tapped delay lines in ice40. I am using (for example)  a delay element as in the attached file delay_element.v. Note that I have applied (* keep *) attributes to all the cells which I need to remain there throughout synthesis.

In yosys I run the script

```
 read_verilog delay_line.v
synth_ice40
stat

   Number of wires:                  9
   Number of wire bits:              9
   Number of public wires:           9
   Number of public wire bits:       9
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                  1
     SB_DFFER                        1

```

As can be seen, the synthesis has optimised away the SB_CARRY (and hence also the SB_LUT4) that I explicitly need to be kept.

Having investigated in a little more detail, when the synth_ice40 command executes the ice40_opt pass, it is optimising away the $__ICE40_CARRY_WRAPPER cell (see int_4.v for netlist after ice40_opt) which was generated by ice40_wrapcarry (see the file int_3.v for netlist after ice40_wrapcarry). 

I think the fault lies in ice40_wrapcarry, as can be seen from int_3.v, there is no (* keep *) attribute on the $__ICE40_CARRY_WRAPPER  cell. I'm not familiar enough with yosys internals to know if this is actually the problem, though.

[delayline.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/3914011/delayline.zip)



## Expected behavior

synthesized netlist contains an SB_CARRY cell, which was marked as (* keep *)

## Actual behavior

synthesized netlist contains only SB_DFF** cell, SB_LUT4 and SB_CARRY have been optimised away.

read_verilog delay_line.v
synth_ice40
stat

   Number of wires:                  9
   Number of wire bits:              9
   Number of public wires:           9
   Number of public wire bits:       9
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                  1
     SB_DFFER                        1
